Olist Intelligence Platform2 / 14
The Problem We Solved
Olist had 99,000+ orders.
But couldn't act on any of it.
👤
Customer Blind Spot
No way to identify which customers were at risk of churning — or had already left
🏪
Seller Gap
No early warning for struggling sellers before they abandon the platform
🗺️
No Regional View
Data scattered across 9 raw files — no consolidated picture of geographic performance
We built a platform to close all three gaps.
Olist Intelligence Platform3 / 14
Our Solution
The End-to-End
Data Pipeline
Medallion Architecture
Raw → 9 staging views → 4 dims + 2 facts + 4 analytical marts
Quality at Every Layer
44 automated tests across 7 GE suites + dbt schema contracts
SCD Type 2 Snapshots
Seller history tracked with dbt snapshots — point-in-time attribution ready
Olist Intelligence Platform4 / 14
ORCHESTRATION
Dagster Orchestration — 8-Asset Pipeline
1 — Ingest
meltano_extract_load pulls raw data from Supabase Postgres into BigQuery olist_raw
2 — Validate
ge_raw_validation runs 44 Great Expectations tests — halts pipeline on failure
3 — Transform
dbt_staging → dbt_snapshot (SCD2) → dbt_marts builds all analytical tables
4 — Publish
Alerts email + dashboard regenerate in parallel, then git push to GitHub Pages
Olist Intelligence Platform5 / 14

Star Schema — Gold Layer

Olist Intelligence Platform7 / 14
Data Quality Assurance
Built-In Validation
at Every Layer
1
Customer ID is Order-Scoped, Not Customer-Scoped
Olist generates a fresh customer_id per order — the same person gets a different ID each time they buy. We resolve this by keying all customer-level analysis (RFM, cohort retention) on customer_unique_id in stg_customers, correctly identifying the same person across multiple orders.
Handled
2
Review ID Duplicates in Source Data
Same review_id appears on different orders — a known Olist dataset anomaly. Handled with composite (review_id, order_id) grain rather than masking the issue.
Documented
3
1 Delivered Order With No Payment Record
Isolated source data gap. Documented in schema.yml, coalesced to 0 in downstream models, and excluded from aggregation tests.
Handled
✓
199 Automated Tests — All Passing
7 Great Expectations suites (44 tests) validate the raw layer. 155 dbt schema contracts (not_null, unique, accepted_values, relationships, dbt_expectations) protect every mart model.
Live
Bad data is blocked at ingest — analysts only ever see clean, tested data ✓
Olist Intelligence Platform8 / 14
Finding #1 — Customer Retention
96.9%
of customers never placed a second order
3.1%
Olist repeat rate
vs
25–40%
Industry benchmark
Converting an extra 5% to repeat buyers = thousands of retained customers at zero acquisition cost
Cohort Retention Heatmap — month 0 = first purchase  |  blue = retained, white = churned
Olist Intelligence Platform9 / 14
Customer Segmentation — mart_rfm_scores
We Know Exactly Who to Target First
🏆 Champions — 6,485
Highest RFM score. Reward & retain.
→ Loyalty reward campaign
💙 Loyal Customers — 27,247
Consistent buyers. Upsell & cross-sell.
→ Nurture & premium campaign
⚠️ At-Risk — 22,282
Previously active, now silent. Win-back window closing.
→ Win-back campaign — act now
💔 Lost — 15,062
Long absent. Heavy incentive or reallocate budget.
→ Reactivation incentive or write-off
Scored: Recency × Frequency × Monetary · auto-assigned campaign_type in mart
Olist Intelligence Platform10 / 14
Finding #2 — Seller Performance · mart_seller_health
18% of sellers made only one sale
Health Score Formula
40% × Avg Review Score (1–5)
35% × On-Time Delivery Rate
25% × Overall Delivery Rate
Composite score 0–100
Four Tiers + Early Warning
🟢 Excellent ≥ 80  |  🟡 Good ≥ 60
🟠 At-Risk ≥ 40  |  🔴 Critical < 40
“Declining” flag fires before seller churns — enables proactive outreach
Olist Intelligence Platform11 / 14
Finding #3 — Geography
São Paulo has 42% of customers. Seller coverage is uneven.
!
Northern States Underserved
PA: 940 customers per seller. AM: highest avg delivery time (26 days)
!
Distance = Late Delivery = Bad Reviews
On-time orders avg 4.2★  |  Late orders avg 2.1★
→
Fix: Local Seller Recruitment
Northeast sellers cut freight cost, delivery time, and late rate simultaneously
Platform late rate: 6.7% — one recruitment push improves three KPIs at once
▶ Live Demo
Switch to browser
maycoooz.github.io/ELT_olist → Geography
Customer Volume — SP dominance
Delivery Performance — late % by state
Expansion Opportunity — customers per seller
Olist Intelligence Platform12 / 14
Business Recommendations
Three Actions to Take Right Now
1
Launch a "Second Purchase" Campaign
Our RFM model identifies every customer in the 30–90 day post-purchase window — the highest-propensity moment for a repeat order. Target with a personalised incentive.
↑ Converting 5% more = thousands of retained customers at zero acquisition cost
2
Proactive Seller Outreach
The health score flags "declining" sellers before they churn. Trigger automated account support, training resources, and promotional tools at the earliest warning signal.
↑ Retaining one at-risk seller preserves their full order volume + avoids recruitment cost
3
Recruit Sellers in the Northeast
Geographic analysis pinpoints PA, AM, and CE as high-demand, low-supply states. Local sellers reduce freight cost, delivery time, and late rates in a growing market.
↑ Better reviews, lower churn, higher satisfaction — one investment, three metrics
Olist Intelligence Platform13 / 14
Built to Scale
This Isn't a One-Time Analysis.
It's a Living System.
🏗️
Medallion Architecture Raw → Staging → Marts. Each layer independently testable and replaceable.
🕰️
SCD Type 2 Seller History dbt snapshot tracks every address change. Point-in-time attribution is ready for live data.
🧪
Automated Quality Gate 44 tests block bad data at every pipeline run. Failures surface before analysts see anything.
📅
Date-Agnostic Pipeline analysis_date variable makes all models work for historical analysis and live feeds with one config change.
⚡
Orchestration Deployed 8-asset Dagster pipeline runs daily at SGT 02:00, fully automated, zero manual steps.
🌐
Dashboard Deployed Self-contained HTML dashboard auto-updates from BigQuery marts on every push to GitHub Pages.
